steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content: A linear function can be written as $f(x)=mx+b$, where $m$ is the slope and $b=f(0)$.

steps.title: Substitute the given information
steps.content: The slope is $51$, so $m=51$. The line passes through $(0,0)$, so $f(0)=0$ and $b=0$. Therefore $$f(x)=51x.$$

steps.title: Choose the match
steps.content: The function $f(x) = 51x$ has slope $51$ and passes through $(0,0)$. The other choices have slopes $34$, $17$, and $1$, so none of them can have slope $51$. A is correct.
